Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

queries.question: Tell me when the trampoline park scene starts
What price is displayed on the coin box of the white laundry machine?
Has the clothing of the person sitting at the white desk changed from a teal and navy blue raglan shirt to a black hooded sweatshirt?

Is the red and white disposable coffee cup with a black lid, previously seen on the desk, still present in the scene where the man wears a light grey t-shirt and uses a laptop with a blue protective case?

samples.output: <think>A man in a grey sweatshirt and headband sits at a desk, pointing at a laptop screen with his right hand while holding a blue pen in his left. A red cup and water bottles sit on the desk near a corkboard covered in photos and notes.</think><action>silent</action>

queries.question: Which social media interface is displayed immediately after the YouTube channel page featuring video titles in Arabic? A. Instagram profile interface B. Facebook profile interface C. Twitter profile interface D. Snapchat profile interface
Is the cork bulletin board with pinned photos located to the right of the person wearing a black hooded sweatshirt?
Is the man wearing a black hoodie smiling at the camera?

samples.output: <think>A man in a grey Nike sweatshirt sits in a parking garage with a black car behind him. He looks to the side and speaks. The scene cuts to a man in a striped shirt holding a white paper indoors, with a lamp and bookshelf visible in the background.</think><action>silent</action>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
